refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety constraints) to enable proper decomposition and allocation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, standard earthmoving task). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) against productivity_target. Cross-check against safety incident log and worksite policy compliance audit.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle count) to enable real-time performance tracking.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop, operator certification) that do not degrade productivity below productivity_target under normal operations.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control system with low-latency operator feedback loop (on-site joystick or remote teleoperation) to minimize cycle time and maximize throughput.
DP-B: Deploy modular task execution engine with pre-programmed dig/load/dump sequences, operator override capability, and real-time productivity telemetry collection and reporting.

DP-C: Implement multi-layer safety architecture (hardware interlocks, firmware governors, policy-based software constraints) that enforces worksite safety rules without blocking nominal task execution paths.

DP-D: Establish integrated telemetry and tasking pipeline (Telemetry interface for metrics, Tasking interface for commands, SafetyAlert interface for constraint enforcement) to unify on-site and remote operator modes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Establish integrated telemetry and tasking pipeline (Telemetry interface for metrics, Tasking interface for commands, SafetyAlert interface for constraint enforcement) to unify on-site and remote operator modes, supported by DP-A (high-performance drivetrain), DP-B (task execution engine), and DP-C (safety architecture). This composite DP addresses the full FR by treating productivity as an emergent property of responsive control, efficient task sequencing, and safety-compliant operation.

potential_sources: Coupling between cycle time (DP-A) and safety constraints (DP-C): aggressive hydraulic response may conflict with emergency stop latency. Mitigation: define acceptable trade-off envelope in safety requirements.

Coupling between telemetry reporting frequency (DP-D) and network bandwidth: high-frequency metrics may saturate Network Link. Mitigation: implement adaptive telemetry sampling based on network availability.

Coupling between operator interface responsiveness (DP-A, DP-D) and teleoperation latency: remote mode may introduce unacceptable delay. Mitigation: define latency budget and validate in teleoperation module.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via interface contracts and trade-off analysis. No fundamental design contradiction identified.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic and control technology without violating safety constraints. Risk: if target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy FR. Mitigation: validate target against historical data and physics-based cycle time model.

Assumption: operator (on-site or remote) has sufficient training and situational awareness to execute tasks safely. Risk: poor operator performance degrades productivity and increases safety incidents. Mitigation: implement operator certification and training validation.

Assumption: worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and non-contradictory with productivity target. Risk: conflicting policies (e.g., speed limit too low) may make target unachievable. Mitigation: conduct policy review and harmonization with customer.

Assumption: Network Link is reliable and has sufficient bandwidth for teleoperation and telemetry. Risk: network outage or congestion degrades remote operation. Mitigation: implement fallback to on-site mode and graceful degradation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-D into explicit sub-DPs for each interface (Telemetry collection, Tasking dispatch, SafetyAlert enforcement) and map to corresponding vehicle subsystems (sensors, actuators, safety controller). This will clarify the physical realization and enable clearer IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry to achieve the productivity KPI. It owns the end-to-end task execution pipeline, operator interface abstraction (on-site vs. remote), and productivity measurement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle count, cycle time, equipment utilization) and operational status (position, orientation, bucket state, engine load) to external monitoring/analytics systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (dig location, load target, dump location, task sequence) from operator (on-site or remote via Teleoperation module) or autonomous task planner.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts (geofence, speed limit, emergency stop, operator certification status) from Safety Services and emits safety-relevant events (constraint violation, emergency stop triggered, operator override) back to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Teleoperation module (remote operator commands and vehicle state) and Safety Services (constraint updates and alerts).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. It owns task execution orchestration, operator interface abstraction, and productivity measurement. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle module owns the physical drivetrain, hydraulics, and sensors that execute tasks and generate productivity metrics. However, productivity KPI also depends on operator control (Teleoperation) and safety constraints (Safety Services), which are outside Vehicle scope. Assigning FR to Vehicle alone would create high diffusion and coupling.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and command dispatch. However, it does not own vehicle control, task execution, or productivity measurement. Assigning FR to Teleoperation would create high diffusion across Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and safety monitoring. However, it does not own task execution or productivity measurement. Assigning FR to Safety Services would create high diffusion and misalign ownership with the primary productivity objective.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because it is the only module that owns the end-to-end task execution pipeline and can integrate vehicle control, operator interface, safety constraints, and productivity measurement into a coherent system. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules or peer modules that contribute to the FR but do not own it.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too many concerns (task orchestration, operator interface, safety, telemetry). Mitigation: clearly define NGHE Solution as an integration/orchestration layer that delegates specific concerns to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services via well-defined interfaces.

Risk: Productivity measurement may be distributed across Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (operator efficiency), and Safety Services (constraint impact). Mitigation: assign explicit ownership of productivity telemetry collection and aggregation to NGHE Solution, with Vehicle and Safety Services providing raw metrics via Telemetry interface.

Risk: Operator interface abstraction (on-site vs. remote) may create coupling between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation. Mitigation: define a clear Tasking interface that abstracts the operator source (on-site joystick or remote command) so NGHE Solution does not need to know the operator mode.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly separate concerns: (1) Task Orchestration Engine (owns task sequencing and cycle time optimization), (2) Operator Interface Abstraction (owns on-site vs. remote mode switching), (3) Productivity Telemetry Aggregator (owns metric collection and reporting). These can be sub-modules within NGHE Solution or separate modules with clear ownership. This will reduce the risk of NGHE Solution becoming a monolithic god module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (integrated telemetry and tasking pipeline with high-performance drivetrain, task execution engine, and safety architecture) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operator to execute tasks with productivity ≥ target while maintaining safety compliance). The DP is logically sound and feasible with current technology. No fundamental design contradiction or infeasibility identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Productivity measurement is distributed across Vehicle (cycle time sensors), Teleoperation (operator command latency), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement impact). Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns the aggregation and reporting of productivity metrics via Telemetry interface.

Diffusion Risk 2: Operator interface abstraction (on-site vs. remote) may require coordination between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation. Mitigation: define a clear Tasking interface that abstracts operator source.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety constraint enforcement (DP-C) may be distributed between Safety Services (policy definition) and Vehicle (hardware/firmware implementation). Mitigation: SafetyAlert interface provides clear contract between Safety Services and NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for the productivity KPI FR. It owns the integration of vehicle control, operator interface, safety constraints, and telemetry. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules or peer modules that contribute to the FR via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No critical containment violation identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is feasible via representative operations trial. Metrics (m³/h, ton/h) can be measured directly from Telemetry interface. Safety compliance can be audited against worksite policy and SafetyAlert logs. Cycle time can be derived from task execution logs. Verification is dependent on clear definition of productivity_target, evaluation period, and representative task profile.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution with sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Each sub-FR has a clear verification method and acceptance criterion. Allocation is traceable from top-level KPI to sub-module responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Explicitly define productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h), evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift), and representative task profile (e.g., standard earthmoving cycle) in the acceptance criteria. This will enable clear verification and reduce ambiguity.

Revision 2: Decompose NGHE Solution IM into sub-modules (Task Orchestration Engine, Operator Interface Abstraction, Productivity Telemetry Aggregator) to reduce the risk of a monolithic god module and clarify ownership boundaries.

Revision 3: Define explicit interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link, including data schemas, latency budgets, and error handling. This will enable clear integration testing and reduce coupling risks.

Revision 4: Conduct a trade-off analysis between cycle time (DP-A), safety constraints (DP-C), and operator interface responsiveness (DP-D) to identify the acceptable envelope for productivity_target. This will validate the feasibility of the DP and reduce the risk of an unachievable target.

decision.justification: The refined FR-NGHE-001 is not atomic and has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (cycle time, operator interface, productivity measurement, safety constraints). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to enable detailed design and implementation. The selected DP is logically sound and the assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is appropriate, but the next level of decomposition is necessary to clarify the detailed design and reduce integration risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle count) to enable real-time performance tracking.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop, operator certification) that do not degrade productivity below productivity_target under normal operations.

assumptions: Assumption 1: productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic and control technology without violating safety constraints.
Assumption 2: operator (on-site or remote) has sufficient training and situational awareness to execute tasks safely.
Assumption 3: worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and non-contradictory with productivity target.
Assumption 4: Network Link is reliable and has sufficient bandwidth for teleoperation and telemetry.
Assumption 5: NGHE Solution can abstract the operator interface (on-site vs. remote) via a clear Tasking interface without creating tight coupling to Teleoperation module.
Assumption 6: productivity metrics can be reliably measured and aggregated from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules.
open_risks: Risk 1: Coupling between cycle time (DP-A) and safety constraints (DP-C) may create unacceptable trade-offs. Mitigation: define acceptable trade-off envelope in safety requirements and validate via simulation.
Risk 2: Teleoperation latency may degrade operator efficiency and reduce productivity below target. Mitigation: define latency budget and validate in teleoperation module.
Risk 3: Network Link reliability and bandwidth may be insufficient for remote operation. Mitigation: implement fallback to on-site mode and graceful degradation.

Risk 4: NGHE Solution may become a monolithic god module with unclear ownership boundaries. Mitigation: decompose into sub-modules (Task Orchestration Engine, Operator Interface Abstraction, Productivity Telemetry Aggregator).

Risk 5: Productivity measurement may be inaccurate or inconsistent across different task types and operating conditions. Mitigation: define clear measurement methodology and validate against historical data.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Historical productivity data from similar equipment and task profiles to validate the feasibility of productivity_target.
Evidence 2: Cycle time model (physics-based or empirical) that relates hydraulic performance, operator efficiency, and safety constraints to cycle time.
Evidence 3: Teleoperation latency measurements and operator efficiency data to validate the impact of remote operation on productivity.
Evidence 4: Network Link specifications (bandwidth, latency, reliability) to validate the feasibility of teleoperation and telemetry.
Evidence 5: Safety constraint definitions and trade-off analysis to validate the compatibility of safety and productivity objectives.
Evidence 6: Operator training and certification requirements to validate the assumption of operator competence.
